expectations.text: Explains all three impact levels: Low, Moderate, High
expectations.evidence: Response has dedicated sections for FedRAMP Low, Moderate, and High, each with definition, characteristics, approximate control count, and example use cases.

expectations.text: References FIPS 199 as the basis for impact categorization
expectations.evidence: Opening section is titled 'The Basis for Impact Level Determination: FIPS 199' and explicitly explains FIPS 199 and its three dimensions (confidentiality, integrity, availability) with the high-water mark rule.

expectations.text: States approximate number of controls for each level
expectations.evidence: Response states ~125 controls for Low, ~325 for Moderate, ~421 for High, and ~47 for LI-SaaS in both prose and a comparison table.

expectations.text: Addresses law enforcement data sensitivity and likely High impact designation
expectations.evidence: Response has a dedicated section 'Your Situation: Law Enforcement Sensitive Data' explicitly stating FedRAMP High is required with multiple reasons, including FIPS 199 confidentiality analysis and practical implications.

expectations.text: Mentions LI-SaaS as a special category for Low impact
expectations.evidence: LI-SaaS is addressed within the Low Impact section with description of criteria (no federal data storage, ~47 controls, limited scope) and noted as a 'narrow set of qualifying SaaS products.'
